text: Although recent studies show an improvement in catalytic performance of iron-based bimetal iron-metal-nitrogen-carbon (FeMNC) catalysts for oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) in acidic condition, there is a lack of fundamental understanding of the roles of the second metal element and the interactions between the two metal elements. We investigate the structure and activity of FeSnNC and FeCoNC by comparing them with their parent iron-based catalysts, i.e., FeNC and SnNC. While the CO cryo-chemisorption shows that the site density of M-N<sub>x</sub> is two times lower in FeSnNC and FeCoNC than those in FeNC and SnNC, the mass activity of the bimetallic catalysts is 50–100% higher than that of FeNC with larger turnover frequency in bimetallic catalysts. The coexistence of Fe-N<sub>x</sub> and Sn-N<sub>x</sub> and Co-N<sub>x</sub> sites in the bimetallic catalysts is confirmed using electron microscopy and X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) techniques. However, no signal of the binuclear Fe-M-N<sub>x</sub> species is observed. Additionally, <sup>57</sup>Fe Mössbauer spectroscopy indicates that the bimetallic catalysts exhibit higher D<sub>1</sub>/D<sub>2</sub> ratio of the spectral peaks corresponding to the Fe-N<sub>x</sub> species than that of the parent FeNC. The second metal element favors more the D<sub>1</sub> site corresponding to higher turnover frequency.
explanation: The text is highly technical with plausible data-tones and domain-specific terminology, suggesting human authorship; however, some awkward phrasing and HTML-encoded subscripts hint at possible AI-generated or machine-translated origin. Overall, the evidence supports a moderate likelihood of AI involvement.
